mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates compress equity valuations by increasing the discount rate for future corporate earnings, generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows, or positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs within one trading week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of threshold-crossing months, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, driving sustained equity appreciation over the subsequent quarter through rising corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods yield negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and margin compression, forecasting negative equity returns over the next month as growth expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: Negative returns do not materialize in >50% of late-cycle production declines, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, generating positive short-term equity momentum as capital flows into cyclical equities.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of housing start increases, or the signal shows zero autocorrelation with market direction across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 3.0 percent forces central banks to accelerate tightening, which suppresses equity valuations over the following quarter through higher borrowing costs and reduced consumer spending power.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >55% of high-inflation threshold months, or the 60-day return distribution shows no significant negative skew relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust job creation, driving positive equity returns over the next month as wage growth supports consumer demand and corporate earnings.
failure_condition: The joint labor improvement signal yields negative 20-day returns in >65% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a recessionary period triggers defensive market positioning, resulting in negative short-term equity returns as investors anticipate prolonged earnings weakness.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of recessionary production drops, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward testing.
